author: Aimé Césaire
verse:
My negritude is not a stone, its deafness hurled against the clamor of the day
my negritude is not a white speck of dead water on the dead eye of earth
my negritude is neither a tower nor a cathedral
it plunges into the red flesh of the soil
it delves into the burning flesh of the sky
it digs through the dark accretions that weigh down its righteous patience
title: Notebook of a Return to the Native Land

author: Delmore Schwartz
verse:
Calmly we walk through this April’s day,
Metropolitan poetry here and there,
In the park sit pauper and rentier,
The screaming children, the motor-car
Fugitive about us, running away,
Between the worker and the millionaire
Number provides all distances,
It is Nineteen Thirty-Seven now,
Many great dears are taken away,
What will become of you and me
(This is the school in which we learn …)
Besides the photo and the memory?
(... that time is the fire in which we burn.)…

Each minute bursts in the burning room,
The great globe reels in the solar fire,
Spinning the trivial and unique away.
(How all things flash! How all things flare!)
What am I now that I was then?
May memory restore again and again
The smallest color of the smallest day:
Time is the school in which we learn,
Time is the fire in which we burn.
title: Calmly We Walk through This April’s Day

author: Edna St. Vincent Millay
title: First Fig

author: Rubén Darío
verse:
Juventud, divino tesoro,
¡ya te vas para no volver!
Cuando quiero llorar, no lloro...
y a veces lloro sin querer.
title: Canción de otoño en primavera

author: Mina Loy
verse:
Spawn of Fantasies
Silting the appraisable
Pig Cupid his rosy snout
Rooting erotic garbage
'Once upon a time’
Pulls a weed white and star-topped
Among wild oats sewn in mucous-membrane

I would an eye in a bengal light
Eternity in a sky-rocket
Constellations in an ocean
Whose rivers run no fresher
Than a trickle of saliva
title: Songs to Joannes: I

author: Léopold Sédar Senghor
verse:
Naked woman, black woman
Clothed with your colour which is life, with your form which is beauty!
In your shadow I have grown up; the gentleness of your hands was laid over my eyes.
And now, high up on the sun-pass, at the heart of summer, at the heart of noon, I come upon you, my Promised Land.
And your beauty strikes me to the heart
like the flash of an eagle.
title: Black Woman

author: Conrad Aiken
title: The House of Dust

author: François Villon
verse:
Dictes moy où, n'en quel pays,
Est Flora la belle Romaine,
Archipiada, ne Thaïs,
Qui fut sa cousine germaine?
Echo, parlant quand bruyt on maine
Dessus riviere ou sus estan,
Qui beauté eut trop plus qu’humaine?
Mais où sont les neiges d’antan?
Inform me where, within what land
Has Flora, lovely Roman, dwelled,
Or Archipiada, or Thaïs,
 Her cousin spawned in Germany?
Or Echo, who responds when called
Across a river or a pond
In beauty's superhuman sheer?
But where the snows of yesteryear?
title: Ballade des dames du temps jadis

author: Geoffrey Chaucer
verse:
A knight ther was, and that a worthy man,
That fro the tyme that he first bigan
To riden out, he loved chivalrie,
Trouthe and honour, fredom and curteisie.
Ful worthy was he in his lordes werre,
And thereto hadde he riden, no man ferre,
As wel in cristendom as in hethenesse,
And evere honóured for his worthynesse.
title: The Canterbury Tales: General Prologue

author: Vladimir Nabokov
verse:
He happens to be a French poet, that thin,
book-carrying man with a bristly gray chin;
you meet him whenever you go
across the bright campus, past ivy-clad walls.
The wind, which is driving him mad (this recalls
a rather good line in Hugo),
keeps making blue holes in the waterproof gloss
of college-bred poplars that rustle and toss
their slippery shadows at pied
young beauties, all legs, as they bicycle through
his shoulder, his armpit, his heart, and the two
big books that are hurting his side.
title: Exile

author: Hart Crane
verse:
And yet this great wink of eternity,
Of rimless floods, unfettered leewardings,
Samite sheeted and processioned where
Her undinal vast belly moonward bends,
Laughing the wrapt inflections of our love;

Take this Sea, whose diapason knells
On scrolls of silver snowy sentences,
The sceptred terror of whose sessions rends
As her demeanors motion well or ill,
All but the pieties of lovers’ hands.
title: Voyages II

author: Julia Vinograd
verse:
Jerusalem dances on living bodies,
on dead bodies, on flesh, on stone
she doesn’t notice, she is not looking down.
'You love death more than me,' God accuses her,
'but I own death,
do you think you can win him away from me?
I give you leave to try,
and if you succeed
will you want my blessing on your marriage?’

’Always,' says Jerusalem, shielding her eyes
against the dazzle of his grief
and searching his face without mercy.
'Death brings you closer for a time
so I can’t refuse his dusty flowers
of blood and pity but his memory’s terrible.’

'So is yours,' God replies like an old chant.
'How many times have you forgotten me?’
'As many times as you remembered me,’ Jerusalem completes it.
'No more, no less.’

She is walking on madmen now,
who weep at their small dirty victories,
snuffling without dignity,
if they could only lose
and be right again,
losers are always right,
whoever wins has a good wife and a home and goes mad.
So goes the legend.
title: The Book of Jerusalem: Death

author: Andrey Beliy
verse:
Hearts are ignited by the sun
The sun expressly to eternity expiring
The sun is an eternal window
To the golden blinding.

A rose sits in the gold of curls.
This rose: how tenderly it sways!
Inside all roses, sunrays' liquid gold
Flows outward with crimson heat.

A pauper heart is filled with evil,
Ground up and burnt;
But our souls are mirrors:
Are reflecting gold!
title: The Sun

author: Lorenz Hart
verse:
My funny valentine,
Sweet comic valentine,
You make me smile with my heart,
Your looks are laughable, unphotographable;
Yet you're my favorite work of art.
title: My Funny Valentine

author: Louis Aragon
verse:
So I am speaking to the past Go ahead and laugh
At the sound of my words if you feel that way
He loved and Was and Came and Caressed
And Waited and Kept watch on the stairs which creaked
Oh violence violence I am a haunted man
And waited and waited bottomless wells
I thought I would die waiting
Silence sharpened pencils in the street
A coughing taxi drove off to die in the dark
And waited and waited smothered voices
In front of the door the language of doors
Hiccup of houses and waited
One after another familiar objects took on
And waited the ghostlike look And waited
Of convicts And waited
And waited God Damn
Escaped from a prison of half-light and suddenly
No Stupid No
Idiot
The shoe crushed the nap of the rug
I barely return
And loved loved loved but you cannot know how much
And loved it's in the past
Loved loved loved loved loved
Oh violence
It's nothing but a joke to those
Who talk as if love were the story of a fling
title: Poem to Shout in the Ruins (translated by Geoffrey Young)

author: Robert Desnos
verse:
J'ai tant rêvé de toi que tu perds ta réalité.
Est-il encore temps d'atteindre ce corps vivant
Et de baiser sur cette bouche la naissance
De la voix qui m'est chère?
J’ai tant rêvé de toi que mes bras habitués, en étreignant ton ombre, à se croiser sur ma poitrine ne se plieraient pas au contour de ton corps, peut-être.
Et que, devant l’apparence réelle de ce qui me hante et me gouverne depuis des jours et des années, je deviendrais une ombre sans doute.
O balances sentimentales.
title: J'ai tant rêvé de toi

author: Ezra Pound
verse:
Gladstone was still respected,
When John Ruskin produced
“Kings Treasuries”; Swinburne
And Rossetti still abused.

Foetid Buchanan lifted up his voice
When that faun’s head of hers
Became a pastime for
Painters and adulterers.

The Burne-Jones cartons
Have preserved her eyes;
Still, at the Tate, they teach
Cophetua to rhapsodize;

Thin like brook-water,
With a vacant gaze.
The English Rubaiyat was still-born
In those days.

The thin, clear gaze, the same
Still darts out faun-like from the half-ruin’d face,
Questing and passive ….
'Ah, poor Jenny’s case' …

Bewildered that a world

Shows no surprise
At her last maquero’s
Adulteries.
title: Yeux Glauques (Hugh Selwyn Mauberley)

author: Thomas Wyatt
verse:
They flee from me that sometime did me seek
With naked foot, stalking in my chamber.
I have seen them gentle, tame, and meek
That now are wild and do not remember
That sometime they put themself in danger
To take bread at my hand; and now they range,
Busily seeking with a continual change.
title: They Flee From Me

author: Innokentiy Annenskiy
verse:
There is a love, and it resembles smoke:
Tucked within — it brings intoxication,
Set it free — and suddenly it's vanished…
Oh, to be like smoke — but be forever young.

There is a love, and it resembles shadows:In the day — lies at your feet, and heeds you well,
In the night — it ever-silently embraces…
Oh, to be like shadows, but together night and day…
title: Two Loves (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Syd Barrett
verse:
Isn't it good to be lost in the wood
Isn't it bad so quiet there, in the wood
Meant even less to me than I thought
With a honey plough of yellow prickly seeds
Clover honey pots and mystic shining feed
Well, the mad cat laughed at the man on the border
Hey ho, huff the Talbot
‘Cheat' he cried shouting kangaroo
It's true in their tree they cried

Please leave us here
Close our eyes to the octopus ride!

The madcap laughed
At the man on the border
Hey ho, huff the Talbot
The winds they blew and the leaves did wag
They'll never put me in their bag
The seas will reach and always seep
So high you go, so low you creep
The wind it blows in tropical heat
The drones they throng on mossy seats
The squeaking door will always creak
Two up, two down we'll never meet
So merrily trip forgo my side

Please leave us here
Close our eyes to the octopus ride!
title: Octopus

author: John Keats
verse:
A thing of beauty is a joy for ever:
Its loveliness increases; it will never
Pass into nothingness; but still will keep
A bower quiet for us, and a sleep
Full of sweet dreams, and health, and quiet breathing.
Therefore, on every morrow, are we wreathing
A flowery band to bind us to the earth,
Spite of despondence, of the inhuman dearth
Of noble natures, of the gloomy days,
Of all the unhealthy and o'er-darkened ways
Made for our searching: yes, in spite of all,
Some shape of beauty moves away the pall
From our dark spirits. Such the sun, the moon,
Trees old and young, sprouting a shady boon
For simple sheep; and such are daffodils
With the green world they live in; and clear rills
That for themselves a cooling covert make
'Gainst the hot season; the mid forest brake,
Rich with a sprinkling of fair musk-rose blooms:
title: Endymion

author: T. S. Eliot
verse:
We are the hollow men
We are the stuffed men
Leaning together
Headpiece filled with straw. Alas!Our dried voices, when
We whisper together
Are quiet and meaningless
As wind in dry grass
Or rats’ feet over broken glass
In our dry cellar

Shape without form, shade without colour,
Paralysed force, gesture without motion;Those who have crossed
With direct eyes, to death’s other Kingdom
Remember us — if at all — not as lost
Violent souls, but only
As the hollow men
The stuffed men.
title: The Hollow Men

author: Kendrick Lamar
verse:
I got this fire burnin' in me from within
Concentrated thoughts on who I used to be, I'm sheddin’ skin
Every day, a new version of me, a third of me demented, cemented in pain
Juggling the pros and cons of fame
I don't know how to make friends, I'm a lonely soul
I recollect this isolation, I was four years old
Truth be told, I've been battling myself
Tryna navigate the real and fake
Cynical about the judgement day
I did past life regression last year and it fucked me up
Reincarnated on this earth for a hundred plus
Body after body, lesson after lesson, let's take it back to Michigan in 1947
My father kicked me out the house 'cause I wouldn't listen to him
I didn't care about his influence, only loved what I was doing
Gifted as a musician, I played guitar on a grand level
The most talented where I'm from, but I had to rebel
And so I'm off in the sunset, searchin' for my place in the world
With my guitar up on my hip as the story unfurled
I found myself with a pocket full of money and a whole lot of respect
While the record business loved me
I was head of rhythm and blues
The women that fell to they feet, so many to choose
But I manipulated power as I lied to the masses
Died with my money, gluttony was too attractive, reincarnated
title: reincarnated

author: Elizabeth Bishop
verse:
The art of losing isn't hard to master;
so many things seem filled with the intent
to be lost that their loss is no disaster.

Lose something every day. Accept the fluster
of lost door keys, the hour badly spent.
The art of losing isn’t hard to master.

Then practice losing farther, losing faster:
places, and names, and where it was you meant
to travel. None of these will bring disaster.
title: One Art

author: José Martí
verse:
A sincere man am I
From the land where palm trees grow,
And I want before I die
My soul's verses to bestow.
I'm a traveller to all parts,
And a newcomer to none:
I am art among the arts,
With the mountains I am one.
I know how to name and class
All the strange flowers that grow;
I know every blade of grass,
Fatal lie and sublime woe.
title: Versos sencillos I

author: Arthur Rimbaud
verse:
And from then on I bathed in the Poem
Of the Sea, infused with stars and lactescent,
Devouring the azure verses; where, like a pale elated
Piece of flotsam, a pensive drowned figure sometimes sinks;

Where, suddenly dyeing the blueness, delirium
And slow rhythms under the streaking of daylight,
Stronger than alcohol, vaster than our lyres,
The bitter redness of love ferments!
title: The Drunken Boat (Wallace Fowlie Translation)

author: Paul Verlaine
verse:
What have you done, you there,
weeping without cease,
tell me, yes you, what have you done
with all your youth?
title: Langueur

author: Allen Ginsberg
verse:
America when will we end the human war?
Go fuck yourself with your atom bomb.
I don't feel good don't bother me.
I won't write my poem till I'm in my right mind.
America when will you be angelic?
When will you take off your clothes?
When will you look at yourself through the grave?
When will you be worthy of your million Trotskyites?
America why are your libraries full of tears?
America when will you send your eggs to India?
I'm sick of your insane demands.
When can I go into the supermarket and buy what I need with my good looks?
America after all it is you and I who are perfect not
the next world.
title: America

author: Basil Bunting
verse:
Let us come upon him first as if in a dream,
anonymous triple presence,
memory made substance and tally of heart’s rot:
then in the waking Now be demonstrable, seem
sole aspect of being’s essence,
coffin to the living touch, self’s Iscariot.
Then he will loath the year’s recurrent long caress
without hope of divorce,
envying idiocy’s apathy or the stress
of definite remorse.
He will lapse into a halflife lest the taut force
of the mind’s eagerness
recall those fiends or new apparitions endorse
his excessive distress.
He will shrink, his manhood leave him, slough self-aware
the last skin of the flayed: despair.
He will nurse his terror carefully, uncertain
even of death’s solace,
impotent to outpace
dispersion of the soul, disruption of the brain.
title: Chorus Of Furies

author: Edgar Allan Poe
verse:
Once upon a midnight dreary, while I pondered, weak and weary,
Over many a quaint and curious volume of forgotten lore
While I nodded, nearly napping, suddenly there came a tapping,
As of some one gently rapping, rapping at my chamber door.
title: The Raven

author: Gertrude Stein
verse:
Why am I if I am uncertain reasons may inclose.
Remain remain propose repose chose.
I call carelessly that the door is open
Which if they may refuse to open
No one can rush to close.
Let them be mine therefor.
Everybody knows that I chose.
Therefor if therefore before I close.
I will therefore offer therefore I offer this.
Which if I refuse to miss may be miss is mine.
I will be well welcome when I come.
Because I am coming.
Certainly I come having come.
These stanzas are done.
title: Stanzas In Meditation: Stanza LXXXIII

author: Patti Smith
verse:
His father died and left him a little farm in New England
All the long black funeral cars left the scene
And the boy was just standing there alone
Looking at the shiny red tractor
Him and his daddy used to sit inside
And circle the blue fields and grease the night
It was as if someone had spread butter
On all the fine points of the stars
'Cause when he looked up they started to slip
Then he put his head in the crux of his arms
And he started to drift, drift to the belly of a ship
Let the ship slide open, and he went inside of it
And saw his daddy behind the control board
Streaming beads of light
He saw his daddy behind the control board
And he was very different tonight\n'Cause he was not human
He was not human
title: Birdland

author: Jules Laforgue
verse:
The one who keeps me informed how a woman feels,
I shall say to her first, with my least frigid air,
'The sum of the angles of a triangle equals
Two right angles, my dear.

And if this cry escapes her: 'God, how I love you!’
'God rewards his own.' Or sadly contemplative:
'Keyboards have a heart. My theme is always of you.’

I: 'All is relative.’

With blazing eyes, aware of being tedious:
'Ah, you don’t love me! But so many others do!’
I with an eye racing toward the Unconscious:'Well enough, thanks. And you?'
title: Another complaint of Lord Pierrot (translated by Louis Simpson)

author: Lou Reed
verse:
The myriad choices of his fate
Set themselves out upon a plate
For him to choose
What had he to lose?

Not a ghost bloodied country
All covered with sleep
Where the black angel did weep
Not an old city street in the east

Gone to choose

And wandering's brother
Walked on through the night
With his hair in his face
On a long splintered cut from the knife of GT

And the rally man's patter ran on through the dawn
Until we said so long
To his skull-shrill yell

Shining brightly red-rimmed
And red-lined with the time
Infused with the choice of the mind
On ice skates scraping chunks
From the bells
title: The Black Angel’s Death Song

author: Langston Hughes
verse:
What happens to a dream deferred?
Does it dry up
Like a raisin in the sun?
Or fester like a sore—
And then run?
Does it stink like rotten meat?
Or crust and sugar over—
like a syrupy sweet?
Maybe it just sags
like a heavy load.
Or does it explode?
title: Dream Deferred

author: Robert Duncan
verse:
Often I am permitted to return to a meadow
as if it were a scene made-up by the mind,
that is not mine, but is a made place,

that is mine, it is so near to the heart,
an eternal pasture folded in all thought
so that there is a hall therein

that is a made place, created by light
wherefrom the shadows that are forms fall.

Wherefrom fall all architectures I am

I say are likenesses of the First Beloved
whose flowers are flames lit to the Lady.
title: Often I Am Permitted to Return to a Meadow

author: Paul Hoover
verse:
They are crying out in restaurants,
so delighted to be speaking,
they appear to be insane.

But we are the silent types,
who hold speech within
like the rustle of gold foil.

We eat our words and swallow hard.
There’s nothing much to say.
The knot’s in its nest, breathing.
A hand thinks it’s a bird.

The world ‘nows'; it doesn’t know.
The world 'wows.' Then it snows.

A word arrives, silent and upright.
It stands in profile against a white wall.
It’s here for safekeeping only.

Keep quiet, mice.
A cat’s patrolling the area,
with drones and more drones.
title: Why is Quiet 'Kept'?

author: Elizabeth Coatsworth
verse:
Cat, if you go outdoors, you must walk in the snow.
You will come back with little white shoes on your feet,
little white shoes of snow that have heels of sleet.
Stay by the fire, my Cat. Lie still, do not go.
See how the flames are leaping and hissing low,
I will bring you a saucer of milk like a marguerite,
so white and so smooth, so spherical and so sweet
stay with me, Cat. Outdoors the wild winds blow.
title: On a Night of Snow

author: Audre Lorde
verse:
For those of us who live at the shoreline
standing upon the constant edges of decision
crucial and alone
for those of us who cannot indulge
the passing dreams of choice
who love in doorways coming and going
in the hours between dawns
looking inward and outward
at once before and after
seeking a now that can breed
futures
like bread in our children’s mouths
so their dreams will not reflect
the death of ours;

For those of us
who were imprinted with fear
like a faint line in the center of our foreheads
learning to be afraid with our mother’s milk
for by this weapon
this illusion of some safety to be found
the heavy-footed hoped to silence us
For all of us
this instant and this triumph
We were never meant to survive.
title: A Litany for Survival

author: Enrique González Martínez
verse:
Twist the neck of the swan with its deceptive plumage
that gives its white note to the blue of the fountain;
he walks his grace no more, but he doesn't feel
the soul of things nor the voice of the landscape.

Flee from all forms and all language
that do not go according to the latent rhythm
of deep life... and adores intensely
life, and may life understand your homage.
title: Tuércele el cuello al cisne

author: William Blake
verse:
Tyger Tyger, burning bright,
In the forests of the night;
What immortal hand or eye,
Could frame thy fearful symmetry?
title: The Tyger

author: Sylvia Plath
verse:
I have done it again.
One year in every ten
I manage it—
A sort of walking miracle, my skin...And there is a charge, a very large charge
For a word or a touch
Or a bit of blood

Or a piece of my hair or my clothes.
So, so, Herr Doktor.
So, Herr Enemy.

I am your opus,
I am your valuable,
The pure gold baby

That melts to a shriek.
I turn and burn.
Do not think I underestimate your great concern.

Ash, ash
You poke and stir.
Flesh, bone, there is nothing there

A cake of soap,
A wedding ring,
A gold filling.

Herr God, Herr Lucifer
Beware
Beware.

Out of the ash
I rise with my red hair
And I eat men like air.
title: Lady Lazarus

author: Percy Bysshe Shelley
verse:
Tell that its sculptor well those passions read
Which yet survive, stamped on these lifeless things,
The hand that mocked them, and the heart that fed;
And on the pedestal, these words appear:My name is Ozymandias, King of Kings;
Look on my Works, ye Mighty, and despair!
Nothing beside remains. Round the decay
Of that colossal Wreck, boundless and bare
The lone and level sands stretch far away.
title: Ozymandias

author: Annie Finch
verse:
I dreamed of a poet who gave me a whale
that shadowed clear pools through the sea-weeded shade.
When beached sea-foam dried on the rocks, it would sail
down currents that gathered to pool and cascade
with turbulent order.
She brims with transparent water,
as mother and poet and daughter.

The surface is broken and arching and full,
impelled by the passions of nation and woman.
The waves build and fall; the deep currents pull
toward rocky pools cupping the salt of the human.
The ocean she's authored
brims, with transparent water,
for poet and mother and daughter.
title: A Carol For Carolyn (in amphibrach)

author: Dora Sigerson Shorter
verse:
Heaven help your home to-night,
MacCormac; for I know
A white witch woman is your bride:
You married for your woe.

You thought her but a simple maid
That roamed the mountain-side;
She put the witch’s glance on you,
And so became your bride.

But I have watched her close and long
And know her all too well;
I never churned before her glance
But evil luck befell.
title: The White Witch

author: Rainer Maria Rilke
verse:
And it was almost a girl, and she came out of
That single blessedness of song and lyre,
And shone clear through her springtime-veil
And made herself a bed inside my hearing.

And slept within me. And her sleep was all:
The trees, each that I admired, those
Perceptible distances, the meadows I felt,
And every wonder that concerned myself.

She slept the world. Singing god, how have you
So perfected her that she made no demand
To first be awake? See, she emerged and slept.
title: Sonnets to Orpheus I.2 (translated by A. S. Kline)

author: Kenneth Rexroth
verse:
Strong ankled, sun burned, almost naked,
The daughters of California
Educate reluctant humanists;
Drive into their skulls with tennis balls
The unhappy realization
That nature is still stronger than man.
The special Hellenic privilege
Of the special intellect seeps out
At last in this irrigated soil.
Sweat of athletes and juice of lovers
Are stronger than Socrates’ hemlock;
And the games of scrupulous Euclid
Vanish in the gymnopaedia.
title: Vitamins And Roughage

author: Boris Poplavskiy
verse:
Jouissance of violets in the basement
Where long dead stars sighed of sepulchral murk
Still phantoms would fling open windows
And morning rose.
They hurt so badly, faces hid away
Until the very dusk
Across its every dimming ray
Forever passed
And in the night, flames sprouted from the walls of homes
Above a void leaned flowers - tempted by the chasm
Below, the Demiurge paced on the asphalt, thinking
How she could ever get inside that wondrous building
Paced so damn long, she'd rest her face on stone
Would softly whisper with her father, cold and gone
Then fall asleep
And one returning from the ball
Would push her with his drunken feet.
title: The Play of Violets in the Basement (Translated by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Bernadette Mayer
verse:
silver and clover the clover
where we sat there over
and over again
and again knee
comes sings a few
things comes
rings a
few things
were settling
the stars
were out
the lines
in the street were about
fines what
about lines
single double triple quadruple
(four times)
what about a double four
times how
about a bass a treble
and silver and gold?
title: Here's Gold

author: E. E. Cummings
verse:
anyone lived in a pretty how town
(with up so floating many bells down)
spring summer autumn winter
he sang his didn’t he danced his did.

Women and men(both little and small)
cared for anyone not at all
they sowed their isn’t they reaped their same
sun moon stars rain

children guessed(but only a few
and down they forgot as up they grew
autumn winter spring summer)
that noone loved him more by more

when by now and tree by leaf
she laughed his joy she cried his grief
bird by snow and stir by still
anyone’s any was all to her

someones married their everyones
laughed their cryings and did their dance
(sleep wake hope and then)they
said their nevers they slept their dream

stars rain sun moon
(and only the snow can begin to explain
how children are apt to forget to remember
with up so floating many bells down)

one day anyone died i guess
(and noone stooped to kiss his face)
busy folk buried them side by side
little by little and was by was

all by all and deep by deep
and more by more they dream their sleep
noone and anyone earth by april
wish by spirit and if by yes.

Women and men(both dong and ding)
summer autumn winter spring
reaped their sowing and went their came
sun moon stars rain
title: [anyone lived in a pretty how town]

author: Wendy Cope
verse:
At lunchtime I bought a huge orange —
The size of it made us all laugh.
I peeled it and shared it with Robert and Dave —
They got quarters and I had a half.
title: The Orange

author: Seamus Heaney
verse:
The baby cooed and laughed and rocked the pram
When I came in, and I was embarrassed
By old men standing up to shake my hand

And tell me they were 'sorry for my trouble'.
Whispers informed strangers I was the eldest,
Away at school, as my mother held my hand

In hers and coughed out angry tearless sighs.
At ten o'clock the ambulance arrived
With the corpse, stanched and bandaged by the nurses.
title: Mid-Term Break

author: Aesop Rock
verse:
I'm twice born, once in 7-something
Once as the resurrection of honorable function
Been shoveling coal as the engine's doctor
Long enough to see my silhouette acquire a permanent kink in the posture
The maintenance of icicle spirit by the warmth of true endearment
Was, is, and forever will be a luxury
I'm a sovereignty columnist, fathering doom document.
Curséd version of a certain Virgin Mary womb occupant
Oh my Lord, what have I gotten myself into?
Seems like I woke up in yesterday again
Another day, another dream
I know swamp rats who never suckled oxygen purification
Sure as blurry mayhem had ‘em spent
Zen freeze
Stuck until my friend leaves, puppet for the plummet committee
Sputtering bum, numb enough to stomach the city
title: Oxygen

author: Momus
verse:
God is a tender pervert, and the angels are voyeurs.
Watching us forever, their vision never blurs
They make us, then forget us for a hundred million years
And then by chance they glance at us and something in them stirs
They find us so provocative, so weak, so full of pride
Our cleverness, our nakedness, fills them with delight
The way we hold our coffee cups, the way we pick our words
God is a tender pervert and the angels are voyeurs
title: The Angels Are Voyeurs

author: Wallace Stevens
verse:
The palm stands on the edge of space.
The wind moves slowly in the branches.
The bird's fire-fangled feathers dangle down.
title: Of Mere Being

author: Lydia Lunch
verse:
You ever wake up with hangover
The size of Mississippi
Feeling like you were broken down
On the wrong side of nightmare alley...
Busted up and thrown up
Tell'em and you can quote me on this
Because I fucking felt like it.
title: Gone City

author: Emily Dickinson
verse:
Hope is the thing with feathers
That perches in the soul,
And sings the tune without the words,
And never stops at all
title: Hope is the thing with feathers

author: Daevid Allen
verse:
Are you able to be free?
Do you know what's on your menu?
Do you understand a tree
Or future creature?
Do you really think you know?
So you think you got the answer?
Don't you think you oughta know
You're just the dancer?
title: So What

author: Ada Limón
verse:
Say tomorrow doesn’t come.
Say the moon becomes an icy pit.
Say the sweet-gum tree is petrified.
Say the sun’s a foul black tire fire.
Say the owl’s eyes are pinpricks.
Say the raccoon’s a hot tar stain.
Say the shirt’s plastic ditch-litter.
Say the kitchen’s a cow’s corpse.
Say we never get to see it: bright
future, stuck like a bum star, never
coming close, never dazzling.
title: The Conditional

author: Omar Khayyam
verse:
Wake! For the Sun, who scattered into flight
The Stars before him from the Field of Night,
Drives Night along with them from Heav'n, and strikes
The Sultan's Turret with a Shaft of Light.
title: The Rubaiyat of Omar Khayyam (Fitzgerald Translation)

author: Edward Fitzgerald
verse:
Thus, then, live I
Till, 'mid all the gloom,
By Heaven! the bold sun
Is with me in the room
Shining, shining!

Then the clouds part,
Swallows soaring between;
The spring is alive,
And the meadows are green!

I jump up like mad,
Break the old pipe in twain,
And away to the meadows,
The meadows again!
title: Old Song

author: Kenneth Koch
verse:
As the adjective is lost in the sentence,
So I am lost in your eyes, ears, nose, and throat
You have enchanted me with a single kiss
Which can never be undone
Until the destruction of language
title: Permanently

author: Diane di Prima
verse:
I have just realized that the stakes are myself
I have no other
ransom money, nothing to break or barter but my life
my spirit measured out, in bits, spread over
the roulette table, I recoup what I can
nothing else to shove under the nose of the maitre de jeu
nothing to thrust out the window, no white flag
title: Revolutionary Letter #1

author: Alfred Tennyson
verse:
O Sorrow, cruel fellowship,
O Priestess in the vaults of Death,
O sweet and bitter in a breath,
What whispers from thy lying lip?

'The stars,' she whispers, 'blindly run;
A web is wov'n across the sky;
From out waste places comes a cry,
And murmurs from the dying sun:

'And all the phantom, Nature, stands
With all the music in her tone,
A hollow echo of my own,
A hollow form with empty hands.'

And shall I take a thing so blind,
Embrace her as my natural good;
Or crush her, like a vice of blood,
Upon the threshold of the mind?
title: In Memoriam A.H.H.

author: Hugh MacDiarmid
verse:
I belong to a different country than yours
And none of my travels have been in the same lands
Save where Arzachel or Langrenus allures
Such spirits as ours, and the Straight Wall stands,
But crossing shear planes extruded in long lines of ridges,
Torsion cylinders, crater rings, and circular seas
And ultra-basic xenoliths that make men look midges
Belong to my quarter as well, and with ease
I too can work in bright green and all the curious interference
Colors that under crossed nicols have a mottled appearance.
title: The Eemis Stane

author: Aleister Crowley
verse:
Io Pan! Io Pan! Come over the sea
From Sicily and from Arcady!
Roaming as Bacchus, with fauns and pards
And nymphs and satyrs for thy guards,
On a milk-white ass, come over the sea
To me, to me,
Come with Apollo in bridal dress
(Spheperdess and pythoness)
Come with Artemis, silken shod,
And wash thy white thigh, beautiful God,
In the moon, of the woods, on the marble mount,
The dimpled dawn of the amber fount!
title: Hymn To Pan

author: Marianne Moore
verse:
Openly, yes,
with the naturalness
of the hippopotamus or the alligator
when it climbs out on the bank to experience the

sun, I do these
things which I do, which please
no one but myself. Now I breathe and now I am sub-
merged; the blemishes stand up and shout when the object

in view was a
renaissance; shall I say
the contrary? The sediment of the river with it and I am myself done away with, for the
patina of circumstance can but enrich what was
title: Black Earth

author: Chuck D
verse:
Banned from our damn so called country
No claim y'all know the goddamn name
Some got the rest of the planet to feel us damn it
Substance over style, that's right we on exile
Them ol' heads from strong out the velt
No love good looking out but damn sure felt
Hear me fear me appeared to disappear
The sequel said keep PE from the people
Stole your soul kept the groove on your body black
Now you can't get your mind back
title: How You Sell Soul To A Soulless People Who Sold Their Soul

author: Francis Ledwidge
verse:
All the dead kings came to me
At Rosnaree, where I was dreaming,
A few stars glimmered through the morn,
And down the thorn the dews were streaming.

And every dead king had a story
Of ancient glory, sweetly told.
It was too early for the lark,
But the starry dark had tints of gold.

I listened to the sorrows three
Of that Eire passed into song.
A cock crowed near a hazel croft,
And up aloft dim larks winged strong.
title: The Larks

author: Scawen Blunt
verse:
Crimes find accomplices, and Murder weapons.
The ways of Statesmen are an easy road.
All swords are theirs, the noblest with the neediest.
And those who serve them best are men of good.

What need to blush, to trifle with dissembling?
A score of honest tongues anon shall swear.
Blood flows. The Senate's self shall spread its mantle
In the world's face, nor own a Caesar there.
title: The Wind

author: Lawrence Ferlinghetti
verse:
The changing light
at San Francisco
is none of your East Coast light
none of your
pearly light of Paris
The light of San Francisco
is a sea light
an island light
And the light of fog
blanketing the hills
drifting in at night
through the Golden Gate
to lie on the city at dawn
title: I Am Waiting

author: Lord Byron
verse:
She walks in beauty, like the night
Of cloudless climes and starry skies;
And all that's best of dark and bright
Meet in her aspect and her eyes:
title: She Walks in Beauty

author: Frank O'Hara
verse:
It's my lunch hour, so I go
for a walk among the hum-colored
cabs. First, down the sidewalk
where laborers feed their dirty
glistening torsos sandwiches
and Coca-Cola, with yellow helmets
on. They protect them from falling
bricks, I guess. Then onto the
avenue where skirts are flipping
above heels and blow up over
grates. The sun is hot, but the
cabs stir up the air. I look
at bargains in wristwatches. There
are cats playing in sawdust.
title: A Step Away From Them

author: Leonard Cohen
verse:
There were three of us this morning
I'm the only one this evening
But I must go on
The frontiers are my prison

Oh, the wind, the wind is blowing
Through the graves the wind is blowing
Freedom soon will come
Then we'll come from the shadows
title: The Partisan

author: Mary Coleridge
verse:
And in her lurid eyes there shone
The dying flame of life's desire,
Made mad because its hope was gone,
And kindled at the leaping fire
Of jealousy and fierce revenge,
And strength that could not change nor tire.
title: The Other Side Of A Mirror

author: Alexander Pushkin
verse:
And banning darkness nightly-swarming
From golden-plated skies above,
Young sunrise to replace the former
Would fleet, the night-hour cut in half.
title: The Bronze Horseman (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Zinaida Shishova
verse:
How strange, oh, God, like under euthanasia,
Thy earth today.
Behind the window, each like an acacia,
The poplars sway.
title: How Strange

author: Velimir Khlebnikov
verse:
Where did he go, unto his end
Would toss the maiden-miscreant
Pine needly boughs, green linden branches,
Severely bitter annotations...
title: Vila and the Leshiy (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Vladimir Mayakovsky
verse:
Can't do it alone
grand piano can't carry
(and even less so,
the fireproof safe).
But if not the safe
nor piano,
how can I
bring over my heart, just to carry it back
title: Impossible (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Vadim Samoylov
verse:
Out here in the night, let’s meet, you and I
Smoke some opium, our treat-read-eat

Out here in the night, let’s meet, you and I
In Chinese to speak indeed
Don’t hide music though, it’s true opium
For no-one else, only for us
Out here in the night, let’s die happily
Playing up some deca-danse...
title: Opium For No-One (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Aleksander Blok
verse:
Above the restaurants every evening
The heated air goes deaf and wild,
And reigns over the yells of drunkards
The musky spirit of spring night.

And far, above the dusty alley,
Above the humdrum summer homes,
A bake-shop’s pretzel speckles golden,
And somewhere sounds a baby’s bawl.

And every night, beyond the crossings,
Still tipping their bowler rims,
Are qualified and seasoned punsters
Among the gutters walking dames.

Above the lake are creaking oarlocks,
Nearby, a female wailing sounds,
And in the sky, to all-accustomed, is
A discus twisting pointless mugs.

And every evening, my sole friend is
A dim reflection in my glass,
And by a cryptic, bitter wetness
Like I, he’s deafened and suppressed.

And near, beside the neighbored tables,
Some sleepy lackeys stick around,
While drunkards with the eyes of rabbits
In vino veritas! – all shout.

And every night, at some odd hour
(Or am I merely dreaming thus?)
Entwined in silks, a female figure
Within the window's fogs would pass.

And slowly, walking past the drunkards,
Never with company, alone,
While breathing through perfumes and hazes,
Beside the window she sits down.

And breezes of primordial legends
The luster of elastic silks,
As does her hat with mournful feathers,
Her narrow hand with many rings.

And chained-up by a stranger closeness,
I peer behind her veil of night,
And there I see a spellbound shoreline
And far, enchanted land I find.
title: The Stranger (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Mikhail Svetlov
verse:
We charged at the foe,
And we camped on the heath,
One song–Little Apple–
Held clutched in our teeth.
A song which still safeguard
To this very day
The malachite steppe
And the newly-mown hay...
title: Grenada (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Elizaveta Akasheva
verse:
Gave out new names — such crude and earthly names —
And augured,
Bright as chrysanthemums, her eyes would laugh away
And summoned.
A fractious woman — not a woman — she was being.
A mystery within Her — endless, lidless — living.
title: In a Chrysanthemum (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Anna Akhmatova
verse:
It wasn't long ago, he was beside me here;
Seemed so in love, and was so tender, so my own,
But it was back in winter, under snow…
And now comes spring, its sighs with poisons veer
It wasn't long ago, he was beside me here
title: I wasn't brought a letter, not today (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Yegor Letov
verse:
Far-away Ophelia laughing in her sleep:
A chubby-bellied thrush, a fuzzy deer
Yesteryear's habitual pencil sketch of snow
Cheerfully crunches thru the teeth

Fancied up Ophelia flowing over borders
Honey snakes, raspberry poisons
A galvanized May, a tiny rubber trolly
A long-expired ticket for a rerun showing

Ophelia was lovestruck, floating far indeed
The earth – it chimed; the night – it beamed
Hours kept on rushing, not scared of any one
Towards their funny ridiculous land

Obedient Ophelia floated to the east...
A marvelous captivity, a granite bliss
A lemony road into woods of orange groves.
A translucent lift out to transcendent floors.

A far-away Ophelia laughing in her sleep:
A willowy thicket, a very tired imp
All the gifted horses rambled off at dawn
Every which way – go catch them if you can!
title: Ophelia (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Anna Barkova
verse:
With the foes on the other side
Is my good old friend.
Oh death, come and fly my way
Out of darling hands
title: Dear Foe (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Viktor Tsoy
verse:
One who at age fifteen ran away from home might
Struggle to vibe with a former boarding school student;
And the one who has a fine existential plan
Seldom considers something entirely different...
title: Boshetoonmy (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Nikolay Komyagin
verse:
Sleeps the giant realm
Evening feels unending
By the belfry, wind
Rises on the Kremlin
title: Apple Orchard (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Alexey Khvostenko
title: Paradise (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Bulat Okudzhava
verse:
Roar of weapons, bullets’ squeal,
Clanging spikes and sabers
All their noises in the chime
Of these drops may vanish
Sunshine, May, Arbat, and love
Loftiest career!
Tincture of the Danish king, drink up, cavaliers!
Tincture of the Danish king, drink up, cavaliers!
title: Tincture of the Danish King (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Osip Mandelshtam
verse:
My beast, my era, who would bear
Within your corneas to glimpse,
And with their blood to glue together
The backbones of two centuries?
Blood is a builder, and she gushes
From every earthly thing, in sprays.
The spineless laggard merely flushes
Upon the doorstep of new days.

A creature must, while life keeps grasping,
A spine-crest carry all the way.
With its transparent backbone cresting
A rolling ocean wave would play.

As if a newborn's tender gristle,
An era of an infant earth’s
Again — all lamb-like, sacrificial,
A crown of life — was given forth.

To pull an era from its prisons,
And to commence a world renewed,
The knees of knotted days together
We have to tether with a flute.

And it's the era that keeps bending
The wave with lonely human angst;
And it's the era's golden standard
The viper breathes out in the grass.

The buds will keep on swelling, soaking,
Then spraying greenery in brines;
And yet your backbone has been broken,
My era — pitiful, sublime!

Now, you peer backwards, cruel and feeble,
And smiles of meaninglessness toss;
Just like a beast, that once was nimble,
Transfixed by trails of its own paws.

Blood is a builder, and she gushes
From every earthly thing, in jets,
And onto shores, with scorching fishes,
Warm gristle of the seas projects;

While from azúre and soggy mountains,
And from a high-up net for birds
Upon your deadliest of bruises
Indifference pours down in spurts.
title: Century (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Mikhail Lermontov
verse:
The waves are playing, wind is whistling,
The mast is bending, and it creaks;
Alas! Not happiness it’s seeking,
Nor from contented bliss it flees!
title: The Sail (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Krovostok
verse:
Turns out God was one mark-ass dummy
Just a fancied-out wee marky toast
Now, where shadows are sliding on gravel
By the breeze every shadow throbs.
title: Lenin (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Konstantin Vaginov
verse:
Out of our eyes of green bloom flowers
And petals are proclaiming thoughts of ours,
A guilty camomile in hand, a crazy Jesus
Roams, guided only by his eyes.
title: Journey Into Chaos (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: W.H. Auden
verse:
O look, look in the mirror,
O look in your distress:
Life remains a blessing
Although you cannot bless.
O stand, stand at the window
As the tears scald and start;
You shall love your crooked neighbour
With your crooked heart.
title: As I Walked Out Last Evening

author: Georgiy Ivanov
verse:
An ordinary day, a normal garden, yes,
But why is it that bells keep ringing everyplace?
And nightingales sing out, and flowers on the snow,
Oh, why, just tell me now, or maybe you don't know?
title: I do not beg for love, I do not sing of spring (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Georgiy Adamovich
verse:
You must unlove the past. And then, in time,
Your love of nature too would disappear
And you'll grow more indifferent each day,
And even more each week, and more each year.
title: Don't speak to anyone. Cease drinking wine… (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Marina Tsvetaeva
verse:
Don't expect me to haunt and threaten
Don't assume that's my grave, these signs!
See, I also was once in love with
Laughing out at such awkward times.

Blood would rush to my skin from laughter,
And my curls in mid-air would weave.
Yet, I too passed along, dear stroller.
Passerby, stop right there, don't leave!

Now, for kicks, pluck a reed stalk hither
Then some berries, it's no great toil.
Won't find strawberries any sweeter
Than these plump ones, from graveyard soil.
title: Passerby (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Afanasy Fet
verse:
On nature ever idly spying,
Forgetting everything, I'm fond
Of watching shooting swallows flying
While evening rolls upon the pond.
title: The Swallows

author: Elena Fanaylova
verse:
The light falls. We smoke in the park.
Yellow comes down from above.
The frozen apple high overhead
Has kept its red.
title: Old New Year (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Boris Poplavsky
verse:
And across the heat, the nightly, hellish, bléssed.
Through the lilac smoke, where clarinet sings,
There would flutter snow, so white and ruthless,
Snow that's fallen millions of years.
title: Black Madonna (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Olga Petrovskaya
verse:
Let us enter as tenderest first violins
These world-wide orchestral revolts.
May our bows, youngest smiles, come in
To this war as our songs’ long swords.
title: We Won't Be Deceived... (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Natalia Azarova
verse:
my bottomless bath-pool
how your mountains and waters brim
jasper pimples swimming
over our ocean of the foot-trod hemisphere
title: Bather-Femme Circle-Faced (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Vladimir Sillov
verse:
From galloping minutes strewing,
I’ll keep only three for myself,
By star-maps estranged, renewing,
New legends will soon descend.

The former ones none remember,
And no one would dare to say:
Of loves I have chugged my ladle,
Who’s there left to love today?!

Twisting, our day comes, reeling
Spirals of loving rays.
Whose heart would you lift up, spearing,
And cover in blood whose brains?

Pluck then, hey! Rip those strings now,
Twining strange legends, stars,
And from them let’s bravely fashion
Chainmail to sheathe our hearts!
title: From galloping minutes strewing (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Margarita Aliger
verse:
And we cannot save her from torture,
Nor feed her some water, nor aid…
Around us the missiles keep scorching.
Then deep silent night. The blockade.
title: Great Expectations (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Ekaterina Sheveleva
verse:
The billboards like a fanning peacock tail would toss.
The billboards – unto the constellation of the Southern Cross.
It’s likely, no billboards are more gaudy than in Hong Kong!
The rabid rainbow over Fuk Wa street.
Leaned on a rainbow storefront stands a girl, a kid:
Her mother’s skirt. Her naked feet.
Under the storefront – rags and wooden boards,
That monstrous sort of housing for the poor.

A woman walks up to the child.
A pair of braids
Remaining of her youth.
The mother, likely…

– Now, beg!, she bids.
Implore and bow.
Show gratitude for mercy.
Beg!.. (No milk left for the youngest!)
Beg!.. (Within the tiny rice pan not a gram!)

Yet, stubbornly, the thin child hand
Stays lowered and uncupped.
Within the young girl’s brows – their wingéd arc –
And in the curving of her lips – are dignity and power –
All that her mother once imprinted on her soul,
All that had since become forgotten by the mother!
title: Girl From Honk-Kong (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Boris Pasternak
verse:
Come thick of the leaf-dropping season,
The last of the geese flocks arise,
Quit getting upset, no good reason:
All fear widens eyes oversized!
title: Hoarfrost (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Paul Haig
verse:
Wish we hadn't smoked another cigarette
The place is near, so what do we get?
The shadow approach is in dress
I like to twist it, that was tin vase
I captured stranger's gaze, afraid to ask

I'd like to walk in town, but is always that smell
A café, where I need a drink to be well
Oh, this is your life, this is so smart
Sleeping in doorways of Coniston hall

Sometimes I know, it's crazy to exist
title: Crazy to Exist

author: Vyacheslav Butusov
verse:
When all the songs I don't remember
Forever grow silent
Into an over-bloomed heaven
Would scream my last departing paper boat!
title: Goodbye America (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Konstantin Kedrov
verse:
HEAVEN – IS THE HEIGHT OF GAZING
GAZE – IS THE DEPTH OF HEAVEN

HURT – IS A TOUCH OF GOD
GOD – IS A TOUCH OF HURT
title: Love Computer (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Polina Barskova
verse:
Evgeniy Schwartz
Hard as if a black quartz
Complex as an illusionist's chest
Freezing over Liteyniy walks past
An Elsinoresembling palace
title: Corner of Liteynaya & Shpalernaya, 1946 (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Fyodor Chistyakov
verse:
Cat and man have trouble tracking days and hours
Big blue heavens swapped for graying ceiling plates
Cat and man go flying, with their nightly powers
But this dream ain’t psychic, and does not elate
title: Man & Cat (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Elena Guro
verse:
Proudly I set on my paths.
Do you believe in me?
My ships are fleeting ahead.
Do you believe in me?
May God provide you a wind at your back.
Under a storm they've crashed
Do you believe in me?
All of my ships are sinking!
And you believe in me!
May God provide you a wind at your back!
title: Proudly I set on my paths... (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Yanka Dyaghileva
verse:
Centen'ry rain
Now words are rambling up a quiet lane
And leafs are crashing down, are fraying creased
The next-to-final verdict gone enforced
All the deposits April raised appraised arise
The dreams suspended on spring-dented ice
Centen'ry rain
Centenary rain
title: Centenary Rain (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Dmitriy Ozerskiy
verse:
And roofs once more beheld the dusk
While building walls recalled the war
And I’m just happy, I’m so blessed,
That someone’s happy

Nothing to fight for
Nothing to dole, it
Boils down to one
Must one get angry?
There, out-front
Bird, I’m a bird now.
title: Bird (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Nadezhda Volpin
verse:
What a shame, what a pain, what a pity!
As if I were the person at fault,
When the big city houses are beating
A lone shelter-less wolf to a pulp.
title: The Wolf (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Rowland S. Howard
verse:
Take the wheel, of the world,
And drive, drive, drive, drive, drive.
Quick as a gun.
Blind like a ghost.
I catch most things in my blood
You all lose between rooms…
One last gasp before I go…

Your crystal skin laced with rust
Laced with rust, laced with rust…
Take the wheel of the world and drive

One blood lust glance of the milky way.
Two shots, one in a million,
Short-circuits a billion light years away…
Down here it rains blood and milk for months,
The zulus are getting too strong to play with no more,
No more, no more…

Take the wheel of the world and drive…Shooting stars!

A pocket too fulluva stars
Too hot to handle alone…
Burning a hole, smouldering footsteps that lead to a heart
Mine, yours, who cares, it still burns,
Still borne,
I'm still burning
Burning down…
Take the wheel of the world and drive...
title: Still Burning

author: Oleg Garkusha
verse:
Where shoulders touch
Heat may catch.
Draw a veil & wait.

Remember life
Smile!
Stow away and wait.
title: Hide Away & Wait (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Yegor Letov (circa Yegor & the Opizdinevshiye)
verse:
Death strolls down the way,
Carries pancakes on a tray,
If you pull one from the stack,
Then that's just your luck!
Death would lift you by your knees,
Plant a burning kiss,
Every penny would fly draining
Right out of your sleeves!

There's a fool roaming through the woods,
Searching for someone still more foolish than that.
There's a fool roaming through the world,
Searching for someone still more foolish than that.
title: About a Fool (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Early Mayakovsky
verse:
White mothers, spasmodically, over black streets
Sprawled out, like on caskets designs.
Wept in to those screaming about battered foes,
Oh, close, close those newspaper eyes!
title: Mama & the Evening Murdered by the Germans (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Leonid Fedorov
verse:
It’s not too late, for we’ve become two now.
We’re still intact, outside there’s howling.
Over the war field shimmers the moon,
Courage is boring.
title: It's Not Too Late (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Peter Doherty
verse:
If Queen Boadicea is long dead and gone
Still then the spirit in her children's children's children, it lives on

But if you've lost your faith in love and music, oh, the end won't be long
Because if it's gone for you, well, I too may lose it and that would be wrong
I've tried so hard to keep myself from falling back into my bad old ways
And it chars my heart to always hear you calling, calling for the good old days
Because there were no good old days
These are the good old days

It's not about tenements and needles
And all the evils in their eyes and the backs of their minds
Daisy chains and school-yard games
And a list of things we said we'd do tomorrow

The Arcadian dream has all fallen through, but the Albion sails on course
Let's man the decks and hoist the rigging because the Pigman's found the source
And there's twelve rude boys on the oars
title: Good Old Days

author: Veronica Tushnova
verse:
They've been so merciful, my years,
I've little cause to rue the fates,
And if a second youth exists
Mine's doubly sweeter than the first.
title: Second Youth (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Aleksandr Skidan
verse:
Unknowingness. Surprise,
That the seen – is mere shadow
of what slides into the slippage
of protraction.
Wilting off
of the echo, bloomed unto aphonia in a crumbled
word. To word, shadow’s
shadow, thought
of the preconceived. In the shade beside
gnarled into death wailing pines.
title: Unknowingness. Surprise (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Jeffrey Lee Pierce
verse:
Oh, Mother of Earth, the blind they call
And yet they stay behind a wall
Their sadness grows like weeds
Upon my thighs and knees

Oh, Mother of Earth, the wind is hot
I tried my best, but I could not
Now my eyes must fade from me
In this open country
title: Mother of Earth”

author: Andrey 'Swine' Panov
verse:
Again a weather barring travel,
But fate not easily escaped,
Our moratorium is blaring —
But USA don't give a shit…
Negotiations in Geneva,
Achieve an impasse again,
Reykjavik really showed the people —
Our Gorbachev the greater man
title: Reagan Provocateur (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Valery Pereleshin
verse:
Links recur in progression
– equal weights, matching forms –
And from their repetition
No great miracle comes.

Chains are placed on a sprocket
which inheres to a wheel,
which is worked by a robot:
50 cents – half-hour deal.

Now, the noggin has factored
all that could be arranged,
the corrosion of metal.
(and defenseless revenge).

Here a spoke gets refinement,
misalignments – repair,
while homunculus-dummies
get retired from wear.
title: Chain (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Pavel Armand
verse:
On the city are storm clouds sweeping. Oh, this city, it reeks of storms.
By the distant, the Narva-side outpost a lonesome young fellow strolls.
Here you are then, my road, far running. Come on darling, I’m here at last.
When we part, you and I, by the doorway, would you wish me some happiness!

Darkest forces are thronging, tossing. At our faces a strong wind blows. To make happiness grow universal fight the workers' battalions.
Come on in then, my friend, my dear. Kissing my lips right away.
And I swear, I shall never forget you, even all the way to the grave.
I am hot with a burning yearning, with such worries my heart would drum.
What’s your name? For in truth, I don’t know you... But our love, it is sure to come!
Come on in then, my dear, my comrade. Get on kissing my lips right away.
And I swear, I shall never forget you, even all the way to the grave.
title: At the Narva-Side Outpost (translated by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Christopher Owens
verse:
Oh, I wish I had a boyfriend!
I wish I had a loving man in my life!
I wish I had a father,
And maybe then I would've turned out right…
But now I'm just crazy
And totally mad!
Yeah, I'm just crazy —
I'm fucked in the head!

But maybe if I really try with all of my heart,
Then I could make a brand new start,
In love with you!

Oh, I wish I had a suntan!
Oh, I wish I had a pizza and a bottle of wine!
Oh, I wish I had a beach house,
Then we could make a big fire every night!
Instead I'm just crazy —
I'm totally mad!
Yeah I'm just crazy —
I'm fucked in the head!

And maybe if I really try with all of my heart,
Then I could make a brand new start,
In love with you!

Come on, come on, come on, come on!

Эх, кабы мне парнишку!
Желаю мужика, да чтоб любил!
И вырос бы с папашей,
Возможно посолидней я бы был…

А ныне я сумасшедший —
Давно сошёл с ума!
Да, я сумасшедший —
Ёбана голова!

Но если всей душою я возьмусь воспрять,
То может всё осилю переиграть,
Любя тебя!

Ох, мечтаю о загаре!
Ох, мечтаю я о пицце и фляге с вином!
Эх, имел бы домик пляжный,
Мы'б проводили ночи за большим костром!

Но я сумасшедший —
Давно сошёл с ума!
Да, я сумасшедший —
Ёбана голова!

Но если всей душою я возьмусь воспрять,
То может всё осилю переиграть,
Любя тебя!

Давай, давай, давай, давай!
title: Lust for Life (English original and Russian translation by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Aleksey Konstantinovich Lozina-Lozinsky
verse:
One day long ago, by the Nile
Together indulged in a dream
One ever-so-lonely old mandril
And a grim dusky hippo with him.
The mandril ached to be a puma;
The hippo: an eagle, well-beaked…
Like you, in great pain they would ponder,
My reader, you dreamer, you freak.
title: By the Nile (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Deadushki
verse:
Rolling on over me again, old stranger night is here,
Gardens of my hermeticism someone had sown with fear,
Yesterday's runner up was shot next to a wall by dawn,
Buried in tabloid basement jails,
Mourned by a songbird flown.
title: Art of Stone Monuments (adapted from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Viktor Sosnora
verse:
Art – is the sanctum of fools,
payback for crucifixion,
the artist – both Caesar and Rubicon
of love and perversion,

athletic, ascetic, ring-stone and foe,
to deaths, hallelujah! –
yells Cain,
who is all at once a spit, a kiss,
and a stone at the gate of Heaven,

is kink-happy swinger, puritan mom,
a commoner mantled in purples,
is masculine master openly femme,
a victim and also rapist.

he’s laudanum, poison, ambrosia and slime,
the Mecca, the truth, the mass,
Is Hermes' grimace, violas and chimes
a vast supernova and germs,

a cutter and lash, of sabers a plough,
o clown of all possible torture,
a hangman, a pilgrim, a target, a gun,
and a bullet hitting the bull’s eye

is Vulcan and Sisyphus, laurels and thorns,
is Maenads and also hyenas,
with gentlest passion for centuries bond
inside of them malice and genius!

now if, flaring axes, falls over this Earth
that first and ultimate Judgement,
and stand before God every bedroom and court,
each architect and every palace

all heresies, shrines, and dogmas, and lies
surrounding complicity's shame...
to God's – Who are You? –,
only artist replies, I – is,
while you all crawl away,

you're blameless, naive, you're hardly so new, –
you’re crowds, merely crowds, you'll get out!
not artists, and they alone get the blame.
each is, in themselves, and that’s all.
title: Art – Is the Sanctum of Fools (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Boris Nesmelov
verse:
The Soviets' homeland stands surrounded
by flaming walls reared by our foe.
But we, encircled, you, beyond it,
today we all – are one, and more.
title: May 1st, 1920 (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Tom Verlaine
verse:
It was a tight toy night, streets so bright
The world so thin between my bones and skin
There stood another person who was a little surprised
To be face to face with a world so alive

I fell ('Did you feel low?') Nah! ('Huh?!')

I fell right into the arms of Venus de Milo

You know, it's all like some new drug
My senses are sharp, my hands are like gloves
Broadway looked so medieval, it seemed to flap like little pages
I fell sideways laughing with a friend from many stages!

How we felt... ('Did you feel low?') Not at all! ('Huh?!')

I fell right into the arms of Venus de Milo.

Suddenly, my eyes felt so soft and shaky!
I knew there was pain...
But pain is not aching!
And Richie: Richie said, 'Hey, man, let's dress up like cops, see what we can do!'
Something...
Something...
Said, 'You'd better not!'

How I felt... ('Did you feel low?') No. ('Huh?!')

I stood up and walked right out of the arms of Venus de Milo!
title: Venus

author: Nikolay Gumilev
verse:
I walked along unfamiliar boulevards,
Suddenly hearing the cackling of crows,
Ringing of lutes, noise of far-away thunders,
And right before me a streetcar arose.

But how I leaped onto stairs of its entrance
Was an enigma; this I couldn't say.
While in the air a bright burnishing pathway
It would trace even in light of the day.

It rushed a blizzard that's wingéd and darkened,
 And it got lost in the void of all time…
Streetcar conductor! Hey, why don't you stop it?!
Hey, can't you stop the streetcar now?

But it's too late. Now’ve we passed by the wall; Then,
Leaping beside a small grove of palms,
Over the Nile, on the Seine, over Neva,
Over three bridges we roared along.

And, having flashed in the car’s glassy window,
Threw us a gaze of long-suffering orbs
A poor old man and, oh yes, the same one
Who died in Beirut a full year ago.

Where am I? Feeling so trapped and so anxious,
I hear my heart in response jabber on:
See that train station? One could've bought there
Tickets to Indias of the soul!

Posters, where lettering, poured out of blood drops,
Voices: out here - in this green space – I know,
Instead of cabbages, instead of turnips,
Heads of dead people are bought and sold.

In his red shirt, with a face like an udder,
Butcher has severed my head, like the rest,
And in a slippery box, by the others,
Right at the bottom my head was placed.

And in an alley with wooden-planked fencing,
I see that house with three windows, gray lawns…
Streetcar conductor, oh, why don't you stop here;
Hey, stop it now! Why, I have to get off!

Masha, my darling, you lived and you sang here,
Weaving a rug for the groom, for me;
Where is your voice now and where is your body?
It cannot be that you died, cannot be?!

But you were coughing, collapsed by the entrance,
While, with a braid over-powdered and plumed,
I went to get introduced to the Empress,
So that I never again met with you.

I understand now: that all of our freedoms,
Only from there, are a light beating through;
People and shades are amassed by the entrance
Into a garden of planets, a zoo.

Suddenly: wind – blowing sweet, well-acquainted –
Over the bridge and right toward me shoots,
In a steel glove, the palm of a rider
And flies a pair of his horse's hooves.

Serving as Orthodox world's righteous castle:
Isaac's Cathedral, in heavens embossed.
There I would pray for the health of my Masha
And for myself sing a Requiem dirge.

Still, unto eons the heart keeps its gloom and
It's hard to breath and it pains to survive…
Sweet darling Masha, but I never knew that
One ever loved, ever anguished this much!
title: The Wayward Streetcar (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Vadim Kuzmin
verse:
Sits leg over leg Chorniy Lukich,
Trigger finger crossed, lips chapped over,
Shriveled old man, peasant robed kid
Sudden green pipes a march start roaring.

We've come from Kronstadt…

By the latrine stands a Latvian gun,
Woolen grey hat, crimson stars shining.
Watching close enough, not to let run.
Tricky out of shirtsleeves nooses twining.

We've come from Kronstadt,
We've come from Kronstadt…

Whitening legs run into the snow,
Body puffing up and everything scrambled,

Springtime coming on; soon, I'll just go!
Brittled voice, an incohereable fragment...

We're from Kronstadt,
We're from Kronstadt…
title: We've Come From Kronstadt (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Yaroslav Smelyakov
verse:
Not far from those little white buildings
Where blooming acacia airs sweet,
A wonderful girl known as Lida
Resides in a house on South Street.

The gold of her hair is well-braided
In tresses like ropes, tightly reeled.
Her dress is of blue cotton fabric,
Where flowers flare, like in a field.
title: A Wonderful Girl Known as Lida (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Sara Teasdale
verse:
There will come soft rains and the smell of the ground,
And swallows circling with their shimmering sound;

And frogs in the pools singing at night,
And wild plum-trees in tremulous white
title: There Will Come Soft Rains

author: Yanka
verse:
Wrapped in bedsheets at dawn over dew in the wind
From infertile ideas to disincarnate guests
From doors sealed all around to wild beasts buried down
From lush fancy buffets straight to bullet-blown heads
title: Out of Big Open Brains (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Ilya Lagutenko
verse:
Run away, down the row a psycho waits for us,
Wants to thread us up onto a hook,
By now, the cuties are divested of charms,
The cars are parked, even gangsters all snooze

Which leaves us the only ones prime for a slaughter-da...
Just a couple of simple...
(and) fresh-faced...
youths.

Ladadadie dadadadadadie
Dada-dada-dadada-da-say...
Run away.

Run away, then he’d razor me up into furs
And the limit would shed every control
Reaching under the thigh, hard as bone
Through a curtain gap watches the squad

Which leaves us the only ones prime for a slaughter-da...
Just a couple of simple...
(and) fresh-faced...
youths.

Ladadadie dadadadadadie
Dada-dada-dadada-da-say...
Run away.
title: Run Away (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Ibrahim Mohammed Saleh
verse:
today we gather altogether 
as the sunbathers
to untether each other
and be the panthers that slinky skip the sky
and separate from fight, uproot the hate in the heart
so unite in the cacophony of right
and be the igniters of light
our diversity in unity
is the spirit of poignancy
that colorizes our dignity
and amplifies the commonalities
we glorify the vacuity of the vivid planet that we called earth
with variety within that creature
which becomes all of nature
as it forms the root of our culture
as the sheer fabric of our caricature
by endowing a uniformity
unto linguistic fragmentation
as the seed of a hope
that cultivates the bloomed flowers
in the heart of diversity
the vitality of our cultural multiplicity
is to be one people with one voice and one face to form peace with unity in the transparent mystery of any or no ethnicity
As we didn't allow some taxonomic trajectory
to render the portraiture of our nature
title: Unity in Diversity

author: Richard Hell
verse:
Time and time again I knew what I was doing and
And time and time again I just made things worse
It seems you see the most of what is really true when
You're stepping into your hearse

Only time can write a song that's really really real
The most a man can do is say the way its playing feels
And know he only knows as much as time to him reveals

And when I want to write a song that says it all at once
Like time sublimely silences the wise
I know that if I try I'm gonna take a fall at once
And splatter there between my lies
title: Time

author: Andrey Knyazev
verse:
The wind was mighty, tearing at the roofs,
And even though it was so late at night,
I doubt too many in that district slept,
The elemental force had really riled up wild.

But all at once, some fellow ran out with a scream,
And with a broom began to swing around,
Oh, wind, you rascal, you disturbed my sleep!
Why don’t you come on out and fight me, now!

I'm not one to tremble,
All things in my reach.
I am strong and nimble,
To the wind a thing I’ll teach.

That’s when the wind began to swirl and toss undone,
And with their roots entire trees to rip.
Where in the world now did this madman come,
Who would proclaim a war against the wind?!

And yet, the kid undaunted, with his broom would swing,
And lure the wind out to the open field.
For truly, he fought well against the wind.
As for the wind; it only grew more mean.

Then, with a whoosh, the broom was carried out of sight,
And right behind it flew the fearless kid.
And after that, more calmly went the night,
Into the dark the loon had disappeared.

The shepherds found him in a haystack come the dawn,
He soundly slept, all furled.
While wind into his ears sang quiet songs,
And gently stroked his curls.
title: A Brave Kid & the Wind (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Ilya Kormiltsev
verse:
She would read the world like a novel
But it was merely a buried memoir
While all of the guys on her block
Stuffed their conscience under each acne scar

To cut through the park with no dog now
May cost more than you’d ever afford
Whenever she's out for too long, her mom dials the local morgue

Her dad fails at locating the doors, coming home,
Then spits into the ready-made dinner.
He says she's as old as her mother –
It's her turn to be married to him now.
The first instance of struggle against sweaty hands –
Becomes only too swift to befall
What is love?
No more than a look from the screen…
Love is simply a face on the wall…

Alain Delon, who’s speaking in French now
Alain Delon is speaking in French now
Alain Delon Alain Delon won't drink triple cologne
Alain Delon Alain Delon drinks bourbon: double iced
Alain Delon is speaking in French now
Alain Delon is francophone

Guys are known to try hard at their lady-friends’ pads
Where she also occasionally comes
Though, for that, in the end, she gets nothing to show,
Except tedious morning dramas
Back at home though, a whole different movie comes on
She stares deeply into his eyes
And more potent than all those who’ll get lucky to know her
Beaming visions reel over her thighs
title: Alain Delon (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Anastasia Evgenievna Kamenskaya
verse:
We will all die. For a miracle pleading,
crowded stand next to the heavenly walls —
animals, all of them better than people,
next to some persons undoubtedly worse.
title: Animals, Who Are Better Than People (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Aleksey Ponizovskiy
verse:
See, lately I've traveled a wonderful land
Where sea myths splash under an amber-like swell
In well-shaded gardens centuries froze
And clouds swim along in flamingo-like rose

In emerald mountains, a glimmering stream
– Sublime as a fable, and deep as a dream –
In yearning to clutch at a shimmering moon,
Waves, reaching for heaven, with gilded-in foam

Wish you’d understand:
I've seen a wonderful land!
You must understand:
It's the most marvelous land!
title: Marvelous Land (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Damon Albarn
verse:
This is the next century
Where the universal's free
You can find it anywhere
Yes, the future has been sold

Every night we're gone
And to karaoke songs
How we like to sing along
Though the words are wrong

It really, really, really could happen
Yes, it really, really, really could happen
When the days they seem to fall through you
Well, just let them go
title: The Universal

author: Boris Grebenshchikov
verse:
From here on out, time will flow in one line;
Step up, or step away – their world's been left behind.
Now I've burned down their life, like a newspaper stack –
Only ash and asphalt remained, but…

Rock and roll's dead, but I'm still alive,
Rock and roll's dead, while I;
Those who still love us, watch us go by.
Rock and roll's dead, while I’m still alive.
title: Rock & Roll's Dead (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Mike Naumenko
verse:
But another new day would put you at ease…
And the evening would bring you a game…
A new day with the same long familiar faces –
Oh, but hasn't this game gotten lame!

Nonetheless – you're the rock & roll star!..
There you are, your old amp is turned on…
And again, and again, and again, and once more,
You are singing those same ancient songs –
That you screamed years ago…

But at night… You’re once more alone…
Hey there, rock & roll star!
title: Rock & Roll Star (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Mahmoud Darwish
verse:
With dynamite she raps her waist…
She explodes…
It is neither death… nor suicide…
Its Gaza's style to announce her worthiness of life…
For four years and Gaza's flesh burst around… bomb's shrapnel…
Neither magic nor miracle…
It is Gaza's weapons and arms for her continued existence…
And the enemy's detritions it is…
For four years the enemy with its dreams rejoicing it had been…
For flirting with time it was fascinated…
Except for in Gaza…
For Gaza far away it is from her relatives…
And with the enemy it is stuck…
For Gaza is a bomb…
When-ever it explodes…
Never she stops from exploding…
The enemy's face she scares…
And from satisfaction with time she repelled them…
Another thing is time in Gaza…
For time in Gaza not a neutral factor it is…
The people it doesn't drive to cold contemplation…
But to the freedom of explosion…
And to collusion with truth…Time there children it doesn't take
from babyhood to old age…
But men it makes of them…
With their first confrontation with the enemy…
In Gaza time is not relaxation…
But storming into the blazing noon…
Values in Gaza differ…
differ
and differ…
Occupied peoples' only value…
Is the extent of their resistance against the enemy…
This is the only competition that is there…
The honorable and hard truth Gaza is addicted to…

Not from books, nor from quick intensive courses…
She learns…
title: Silence For Gaza

author: Zemfira
verse:
Forever youthful one,
Over the dunes you'll find
Awaits your galleon
That I once fantasized
Twenty-two months that pass
Pigeons keep making fuss
People say bye, then turn
And never again return!
title: Don't You Let Go (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: John Cale
verse:
But us, like other angry whores
Discuss what threats were made before
You don't need them anymore
You know more than I know

Instead, I read the morning news
In bed – what endlessness ahead
And there's no more to be said
You know more than I know

The blind may see, but stay behind relief
Of all liability and greed
And there's nothing more you need
You know more than I know

No one listens to it
They don't believe it
But it's the only way for me
You know more than I know

What crap, old chap fills up the gap
We set like traps, like traps for us, the rats
And there's nothin' more to catch
You know more than I know

Then bury me deep down among the weeds
That creep into the hearts of all the weak
And there's nothing more so weak
You know more than I know
title: You Know More Than I Know

author: Joseph Brodsky
verse:
Behind train depots’ frightening chandeliers
Shoving others, and making shades flee,
Behind threefold emotions, belated ones,
You are presently living from me.

Behind love, behind duty, courageousness
Further yet - past that face all your own,
Past that river, enlightened through marriages,
Behind one who would swim all alone.
title: To A.A. Akhmatova (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Arkadiy Ostrovskiy
verse:
Won't you seek me out a moonstone
As a token of our love
Under earth, or up in heavens,
Far away, or high above

It's not true that it brings curses
It's a lucky sort of stone
Split the moon up into pieces
For the lovers down below
title: Moonstone (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Scott Walker
verse:
Do I hear 21, 21, 21?
I'll give you 21, 21, 21

This night you are mistaken
I'm a farmer in the city
Dark farm houses 'gainst the sky
Every night I must wonder why
Harness on the left nail
Keeps wrinkling, wrinkling
Then higher above me
Esau, Esau
Can't go by a man from Rio
Go by a man from Vigo
Can't go by a man from Ostia
Hey, Ninetto
Remember that dream?
We talked about it so many times
title: Farmer in the City

author: Nikolai Dobronravov
verse:
Feels so barren without you on Earth…
How to live through just a few more hours?
Garden leafs keep falling, as before,
And the taxi cars run off someplace…
But it's empty on this earth alone,
Without you, while you are in flight
And the stars, they all give you their dearness…
title: Dearness (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Svetlana Chapurina
verse:
This Mole,
Who wasn’t vain at all,
But sweet in all the ways;
Who was clearly raised by mommy.

Oh, mole!
Then everyone who will
Stomped down on his back,
Bashed him on the head and tummy…

 Mole!
But how out of luck, how fucked, how unfortunate
All the ones to whom he would send an air kiss…
From without!
title: Mole (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Alexander Bashlachev
verse:
When we’d weep – didn’t spare on salt, did we.
When we'd feast – only glazed and sugar'd cakes.
Calloused-black fingers of bell-ringers ripped
Bronzéd nerves of the amplifier cabs.
title: Age of Small Bells Chiming (translated from Russian by Aleksey Calvin)

author: Irina Odoevtseva
verse:
Now, a pharmacy cross is beaming
Green — like hope, every window greening,
Like my blanket, my fluffy, green.
I am shutting, like blinds, my eyelids,
